parts.title: The Shy Explorer
parts.text: Maya had always been naturally **timid** and got nervous around new people. But she had a special **ability** to read maps better than anyone her age. Her teacher told her it was a **custom** in many cultures to honor those who were brave enough to travel into unknown lands.

parts.title: The Expedition
parts.text: One summer, Maya joined an expedition to a **remote** mountain region where almost no one lived. The **climate** there was cold and unpredictable, with sudden snowstorms that rolled in without warning. She had to **avoid** the icy patches on the trail so she would not slip. Deep in the forest, the team discovered a cave where the walls had begun to **decay** from years of moisture.

parts.title: The Presentation
parts.text: Inside, they found ancient carvings never seen before. Maya was careful not to **disturb** any of the artifacts by touching them carelessly. The team decided to **expose** their findings at a science fair so others could learn. Maya, who used to **cling** to her comfort zone, finally felt ready to step into the spotlight. She was too **bashful** to shout, but when it was her turn to **perform** in front of the crowd, she spoke clearly and confidently.

parts.title: The Result
parts.text: Maya's presentation won first place at the science fair. Her teacher beamed with pride and said she had never seen a student grow so much in one summer. Maya realized that being quiet was not a weakness — it just meant she observed more before she spoke. She began planning her next expedition before the trophy was even engraved.

definitions.meaning1: The usual weather conditions of a place over a long period of time. Example: The climate in the desert is very hot and dry.
definitions.meaning2: The general mood or feeling of a situation. Example: The climate at school improved after the new rules were introduced.
definitions.studentChallenge: Scientists are studying how the ___ is changing due to rising temperatures.

definitions.meaning1: To hold on tightly to something. Example: The frightened child would cling to the railing as she crossed the bridge.
definitions.studentChallenge: The baby koala would ___ to its mother's back wherever she went.

definitions.meaning1: A traditional way of behaving that has been practiced for a long time. Example: It is a custom in our family to eat a big breakfast every Sunday.
definitions.studentChallenge: Wearing a costume during the harvest festival is an old ___ in that village.

definitions.meaning1: To rot or break down over time. Example: The fallen log began to decay after years of sitting in the wet forest.
definitions.meaning2: The process of rotting. Example: Tooth decay can be prevented by brushing regularly.
definitions.studentChallenge: Without proper care, old buildings will slowly ___ and crumble.

definitions.meaning1: To interrupt or bother someone or something. Example: Please do not disturb the sleeping baby.
definitions.meaning2: To move something from its place. Example: The loud noise disturbed the birds in the nest.
definitions.studentChallenge: The loud construction noise began to ___ the animals living nearby.

definitions.meaning1: To uncover or make visible. Example: The scientist decided to expose the old bones to sunlight.
definitions.meaning2: To make known; to reveal. Example: The reporter wanted to expose the truth about the company.
definitions.studentChallenge: Removing the bandage will ___ the wound to fresh air so it can heal.

definitions.meaning1: To carry out or do something, especially in front of an audience. Example: She practiced every day so she could perform the violin solo perfectly.
definitions.studentChallenge: The students were excited to ___ their play for the entire school.

definitions.meaning1: Far away from towns or cities. Example: The cabin was so remote that the nearest town was three hours away.
definitions.studentChallenge: Scientists use special instruments to study ___ areas at the bottom of the ocean.

definitions.meaning1: Shy and easily frightened. Example: The timid deer stood at the edge of the woods and would not come closer.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ mouse crept slowly toward the cheese, ready to run at any sound.

definitions.meaning1: The skill or power to do something. Example: She has the ability to solve difficult math problems faster than anyone.
definitions.studentChallenge: Learning a new language takes time, but most children have the ___ to do it.

definitions.meaning1: To stay away from something. Example: She tried to avoid the icy sidewalk by walking on the grass.
definitions.studentChallenge: The doctor told him to ___ spicy food until his stomach felt better.

definitions.meaning1: Shy and easily embarrassed. Example: The bashful girl hid behind her mother when strangers came to the door.
definitions.studentChallenge: He was too ___ to raise his hand, even though he knew the right answer.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The scientist decided to _______ the old bones to sunlight to study how they changed over time.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The reporter did not want to _______ the location of the secret meeting to anyone.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The baby koala would _______ to its mother's back wherever she went in the forest.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The frightened child would _______ to the railing as she crossed the wobbly bridge.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The fallen log began to _______ after years of sitting in the wet forest.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Without proper care, your teeth will _______ and cause painful cavities.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: It is a _______ in our family to eat a big breakfast every Sunday morning.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Wearing a costume during the harvest festival is an old _______ in that village.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Please do not _______ the sleeping baby when you walk through the room.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The loud construction noise began to _______ the birds nesting in the nearby trees.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The cabin was so _______ that the nearest town was three hours away by car.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Scientists use special instruments to study _______ areas at the bottom of the ocean.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ in the desert is very hot and dry for most of the year.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Scientists are studying how the _______ is changing due to rising temperatures around the world.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She has the _______ to solve difficult math problems faster than anyone else in her class.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Learning a new language takes time, but most children have the _______ to do it.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ deer stood at the edge of the woods and would not come any closer.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The _______ mouse crept slowly toward the cheese, ready to run at the tiniest sound.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She tried to _______ the icy sidewalk by walking on the grass instead.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The doctor told him to _______ spicy food until his stomach felt better.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The students were excited to _______ their play for the entire school on Friday afternoon.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She practiced every day so she could _______ the difficult violin solo perfectly at the concert.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ girl hid behind her mother when strangers came to the front door.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: He was too _______ to raise his hand in class, even though he knew the right answer.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
Maya had always been naturally _(1)_ and got nervous around new people. But she had a special _(2)_ to read maps better than anyone her age. Her teacher told her it was a _(3)_ in many cultures to honor those who were brave enough to travel into unknown lands.

One summer, Maya joined an expedition to a _(4)_ mountain region where almost no one lived. The _(5)_ there was cold and unpredictable, with sudden snowstorms that rolled in without warning. She had to _(6)_ the icy patches on the trail so she would not slip.

Deep in the forest, the team discovered a cave where the walls had begun to _(7)_ from years of moisture. Inside, they found ancient carvings never seen before. Maya was careful not to _(8)_ any of the artifacts by touching them carelessly.

The team decided to _(9)_ their findings at a science fair so others could learn about the discovery. Maya, who used to _(10)_ to her comfort zone, finally felt ready to step into the spotlight. She was too _(11)_ to shout, but when it was her turn to _(12)_ in front of the crowd, she spoke clearly and confidently.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
